source.title: Add Unit Economics pipeline Grafana dashboards + freshness/completeness alerts
source.author: Aisha Bello
source.release_notes: Internal-only: Adds Grafana dashboards and alert rules for the Unit Economics pipeline (freshness/completeness/dimension coverage) with runbook links and tuned severities.
source.body:
## Summary
Adds internal Grafana dashboards and alert rules for the Unit Economics pipeline (hourly aggregation + warehouse views) so we can detect missing hours, freshness lag, and dimension coverage regressions before Finance/Product consume the Console dashboard.

This PR lives in `observability-pack` and only ships dashboards/alerts (no runtime or pipeline code changes). It assumes the aggregator and DQ checks emit the metrics introduced in the Redwood monorepo work (ENG-8426/ENG-8429).

## Motivation
During dogfood we repeatedly saw "looks like a cost spike" that was actually missing output tokens or delayed aggregation. The Console page is not the right place to diagnose pipeline health, and SRE asked for a standard set of panels + alerts aligned to the proposed SLO.

## What’s included
- Grafana dashboard: **Unit Economics — Pipeline Health**
  - Freshness lag (now - last successful hourly partition) by environment
  - Ingestion completeness (% expected hourly partitions present in last 24h)
  - Aggregation job runtime and failure rate
  - Warehouse view query latency (p95/p99) for the dashboard endpoints
  - Backfill activity (rows written/hour + throttling indicators)
- Grafana dashboard: **Unit Economics — Coverage & Drift**
  - Output token totals by hour with 7d baseline band (detect sudden drops)
  - Dimension coverage panels:
    - % rows missing `region`
    - % rows missing `customer_tier`
    - % rows missing `model_id` / `model_version`
    - `quantization_profile` cardinality + unknown/other bucket rate
  - Top offenders tables (regions with high null mapping, tiers with zero coverage)
- Alert rules (Prometheus-style via Jsonnet)
  - `unit_econ_pipeline_freshness_lag_hours > 2` (page)
  - `unit_econ_pipeline_missing_partitions_24h > 1` (page)
  - `unit_econ_output_tokens_drop_ratio < 0.6` vs trailing 7d median (page; 2 consecutive evals)
  - `unit_econ_dimension_null_rate{dim=region} > 0.5%` (ticket)
  - `unit_econ_dimension_null_rate{dim=customer_tier} > 0.5%` (ticket)
  - `unit_econ_quant_profile_unknown_rate > 0.2%` (ticket)
  - `unit_econ_warehouse_view_latency_p99_ms > 2500` (ticket)
- Alert routing + runbook links
  - Pages route to `#eng-sre` and `#eng-platform` with oncall mention
  - Tickets route to `#eng-platform` with a lower-noise policy
  - Runbook URL is included in annotations and points to the unit economics oncall guide (ENG-8435)

## Notes on thresholds / noise
- Freshness/page thresholds mirror the proposed SLO: "hourly data available within 90 minutes" with a buffer for warehouse maintenance windows.
- Token-drop alert uses a ratio vs trailing 7d median per environment, because absolute tokens vary substantially by weekday and launches.
- Dimension null-rate thresholds are set to 0.5% for now to catch mapping regressions without paging on small blips. We can tighten after 2 weeks of baseline.

## Files / areas changed (high level)
- `dashboards/unit-economics/pipeline-health.jsonnet` (new)
- `dashboards/unit-economics/coverage-and-drift.jsonnet` (new)
- `alerts/unit-economics/pipeline-alerts.jsonnet` (new)
- `alerts/unit-economics/README.md` (new; runbook + alert intent)
- `lib/panels/unit-economics.libsonnet` (new; shared panels + variable definitions)
- `team-ownership/oncall-rotations.libsonnet` (updated routing for econ pipeline)

## Example rule (illustrative)
The freshness alert is keyed off the "last successful hourly partition" metric exported by the aggregator job:
- expr: `time() - unit_econ_agg_last_success_timestamp_seconds > 2 * 3600`
- for: `10m`
- severity: page
- annotations include runbook and a "first checks" list.

## Testing / verification
- Rendered dashboards locally with `make grafana-render` and verified variable wiring (env/cluster/warehouse).
- Loaded into staging Grafana and confirmed panels populate for the last 24h.
- Dry-ran alerts in staging with lowered thresholds to confirm routing + annotations.

## Rollout plan
1) Merge and deploy observability-pack.
2) Enable alerts in staging for 24h.
3) Enable production alerts with `ticket` severities only for 48h (collect baseline).
4) Promote freshness + missing partitions + token-drop to `page`.

## Open questions
- Do we want a separate Finance-friendly "health badge" summary panel embedded in the Console dashboard? (out of scope here)
- Should `unit_econ_warehouse_view_latency_*` alerts be owned by Console oncall instead of Platform? Current routing goes to Platform due to materialization/partitioning dependencies.

## PR conversation highlights
- Allison Grant: Requested that paging alerts include direct runbook links and a short decision tree. Updated annotations to include: verify ingestion lag, check latest partitions table, run backfill if needed.
- Sean Gallagher: Asked to avoid paging on single-hour drops during scheduled warehouse maintenance. Added `for: 10m` and excluded known maintenance window label where present.
- Caleb Johnson: Flagged that `quantization_profile` could become high-cardinality; requested a dedicated panel + alert on unknown/other bucket rate rather than raw cardinality alone. Added both.
- Mei Lin: Noted the p99 latency threshold should be environment-specific. Added separate thresholds: staging 4000ms ticket, prod 2500ms ticket.

## CI
- lint/jsonnet: pass
- dashboards render: pass
- unit tests (alerts schema validation): pass

Merged via squash.

Refs: ENG-8429 (data quality checks/anomaly alerts), ENG-8435 (oncall runbook/playbook docs).

source.title: Add dashboards + alerts for data residency enforcement (blocked requests, policy eval latency)
source.author: Allison Grant
source.release_notes:
- New Grafana dashboard: Data Residency Enforcement (overview + drilldowns)
- New alert rules for elevated blocked request rate and policy evaluation latency regression
- Added runbook links + example queries to dashboard panels
source.description: Summary: This PR adds a first-pass set of dashboards and alerting rules for Hosted data residency enforcement. The focus is on (1) visibility into enforcement outcomes (allowed vs blocked), (2) early detection of regressions in policy evaluation latency, and (3) detecting cross-region routing attempts that should be prevented once enforcement is enabled. Context: As part of Hosted data residency enforcement rollout (region pinning + deterministic request-time blocking), SRE and Security asked for a consistent set of metrics visualizations and actionable alerts. This pack is intended to be deployed alongside the routing/gateway changes and used during dogfood and progressive rollout. What’s included: Grafana dashboard: “Data Residency Enforcement” with panels: Overview: total requests, allowed/blocked, block-rate (%), and top block reasons (5m/1h). Blocked requests by region + endpoint: breakdown by edge region, model route, and request_type (streaming vs non-streaming). Blocked requests by org/project (top N): uses org_id/project_id labels when present; falls back to org_tier if IDs are intentionally redacted in some environments. Policy evaluation latency: p50/p95/p99 over time from residency policy decision histogram. Routing attempts: cross-region routing attempt counter and fallback suppression counter (to validate that fallback is being blocked unless explicitly allowed). Error contract adoption: panel showing residency_violation responses vs generic 5xx on streaming path (to catch the SUP-28577 class of issues). Alerts: 1) residency.block_rate_high (severity=page): triggers when blocked requests spike above expected baseline after filtering out known test orgs. Default threshold: > 2% block-rate AND > 30 blocked requests/min for 10m, grouped by region. 2) residency.block_rate_high_ticket (severity=ticket): triggers at lower thresholds to create early-warning noise without paging. Default threshold: > 0.5% AND > 10/min for 15m. 3) residency.policy_eval_latency_p95 (severity=page): p95 residency decision latency > 25ms for 10m, grouped by region (catches cache misses, policy service slowness, or routing layer regressions). 4) residency.cross_region_attempts (severity=page): any sustained non-zero cross-region routing attempt rate (> 1/min for 10m) after enforcement is enabled (gated via a deployment label so we don’t page during pre-enforcement dry runs). Example queries (PromQL) used in panels/alerts: Block rate (region): sum(rate(redwood_residency_enforcement_decision_total{decision="blocked"}[5m])) by (region) / clamp_min(sum(rate(redwood_residency_enforcement_decision_total[5m])) by (region), 1) Top block reasons: topk(10, sum(rate(redwood_residency_enforcement_decision_total{decision="blocked"}[15m])) by (block_reason)) Policy eval p95 (region): histogram_quantile(0.95, sum(rate(redwood_residency_policy_eval_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])) by (le, region)) Cross-region attempts: sum(rate(redwood_residency_cross_region_routing_attempt_total[5m])) by (src_region, dst_region) Notes/assumptions: Metric names match the current instrumentation plan in routing/gateway branches: redwood_residency_enforcement_decision_total (labels: decision, block_reason, region, endpoint, request_type, org_tier, org_id?, project_id?, policy_id?) redwood_residency_policy_eval_duration_seconds_bucket redwood_residency_cross_region_routing_attempt_total redwood_residency_fallback_suppressed_total If labels diverge slightly in final implementation, we can follow up with a small patch; this PR keeps queries isolated in jsonnet so updates are straightforward. Files changed (high level): dashboards/residency/enforcement-overview.jsonnet (new) dashboards/residency/enforcement-drilldowns.jsonnet (new) dashboards/residency/enforcement-errors.jsonnet (new) alerts/residency-enforcement.rules.yml (new) mixins/residency/README.md (new runbook links + definitions) dashboards/_index.jsonnet (register new dashboard) alerts/_index.yml (register new rules) Review notes: Sean Gallagher (SRE): Requested separating page vs ticket alerts and adding minimum volume gates to avoid paging during low-traffic regions. Addressed by adding dual alerts and clamp/volume checks; also added a panel for raw blocked count next to percentage. Rafael Mendes (SRE Manager): Asked for explicit links to runbooks and to tag alerts with the rollout phase label so we can disable paging during Phase 2 staging validation. Added runbook URLs and a required label residency_enforcement_enabled="true" to paging alerts where applicable. Logan Wright (Telemetry/audit): Confirmed that org_id/project_id labels are acceptable in internal metrics; requested a guard to allow redaction in some environments. Implemented by building panels that work with org_tier and optional org_id/project_id. Mina Haddad (Routing): Verified cross-region attempt panels align with how routing will emit src/dst labels; requested a panel to show fallback suppressed vs fallback attempted. Added a small comparison panel. Testing/verification: Rendered dashboards via jsonnet build locally; validated against staging Prometheus with synthetic counters from the residency canary job. Confirmed alert rules load successfully and evaluate with expected firing behavior when replaying a spike scenario. CI: lint-jsonnet: pass prometheus-rules-validate: pass dashboard-render-smoke: pass Outcome: Merged (squash) after approvals; safe to deploy with the next observability-pack rollout. Follow-ups: (1) Once gateway error contract is fully rolled out on streaming endpoints, we should tighten the “generic 5xx on streaming” panel and convert it into an alert for regressions (tracked in ENG-4833). (2) Consider adding an SLO panel (block-rate budget) per allowlisted enterprise org during Phase 3 rollout.
commits: dashboards(residency): add enforcement overview panels (blocked vs allowed, reasons, regions)
alerts(residency): add page + ticket rules for block-rate and policy eval latency
dashboards(residency): add cross-region attempt + fallback suppressed panels
docs(mixins): add metric definitions + runbook links for residency enforcement
source.conversation: Review conversation (selected): Sean Gallagher: The initial block-rate alert as paging will be noisy during rollout. Can we add a min-volume gate and split into page vs ticket? Allison Grant: Yep. I added clamp_min on denominator + an absolute blocked/min threshold and created a ticket-level alert with lower sensitivity. Paging alert now requires residency_enforcement_enabled="true". Rafael Mendes: Please add runbook links in alert annotations + a note on how to disable paging during Phase 2. Allison Grant: Added annotations linking to the residency enforcement false positives runbook and added guidance in mixins/residency/README.md. Also gated the paging alerts on residency_enforcement_enabled. Logan Wright: Any concerns about org_id/project_id being emitted in metrics for Hosted? Security asked about redaction in some contexts. Allison Grant: Dashboard works with org_tier alone; org_id/project_id panels are optional and only appear when labels exist. We can additionally strip labels in restricted environments without breaking the baseline panels. Mina Haddad: Cross-region attempts panel should show src/dst regions so we can see where fallbacks were attempted. Also would be good to compare fallback_suppressed vs cross_region_attempt. Allison Grant: Added src_region/dst_region breakdown and a side-by-side panel.

The focus is on (1) visibility into enforcement outcomes (allowed vs blocked), (2) early detection of regressions in policy evaluation latency, and (3) detecting cross-region routing attempts that should be prevented once enforcement is enabled. (2) Consider adding an SLO panel (block-rate budget) per allowlisted enterprise org during Phase 3 rollout.
text: Summary: This PR adds a first-pass set of dashboards and alerting rules for Hosted data residency enforcement. The focus is on (1) visibility into enforcement outcomes (allowed vs blocked), (2) early detection of regressions in policy evaluation latency, and (3) detecting cross-region routing attempts that should be prevented once enforcement is enabled. Context: As part of Hosted data residency enforcement rollout (region pinning + deterministic request-time blocking), SRE and Security asked for a consistent set of metrics visualizations and actionable alerts. This pack is intended to be deployed alongside the routing/gateway changes and used during dogfood and progressive rollout. What’s included: Grafana dashboard: “Data Residency Enforcement” with panels: Overview: total requests, allowed/blocked, block-rate (%), and top block reasons (5m/1h). Blocked requests by region + endpoint: breakdown by edge region, model route, and request_type (streaming vs non-streaming). Blocked requests by org/project (top N): uses org_id/project_id labels when present; falls back to org_tier if IDs are intentionally redacted in some environments. Policy evaluation latency: p50/p95/p99 over time from residency policy decision histogram. Routing attempts: cross-region routing attempt counter and fallback suppression counter (to validate that fallback is being blocked unless explicitly allowed). Error contract adoption: panel showing residency_violation responses vs generic 5xx on streaming path (to catch the SUP-28577 class of issues). Alerts: 1) residency.block_rate_high (severity=page): triggers when blocked requests spike above expected baseline after filtering out known test orgs. Default threshold: > 2% block-rate AND > 30 blocked requests/min for 10m, grouped by region. 2) residency.block_rate_high_ticket (severity=ticket): triggers at lower thresholds to create early-warning noise without paging. Default threshold: > 0.5% AND > 10/min for 15m. 3) residency.policy_eval_latency_p95 (severity=page): p95 residency decision latency > 25ms for 10m, grouped by region (catches cache misses, policy service slowness, or routing layer regressions). 4) residency.cross_region_attempts (severity=page): any sustained non-zero cross-region routing attempt rate (> 1/min for 10m) after enforcement is enabled (gated via a deployment label so we don’t page during pre-enforcement dry runs). Example queries (PromQL) used in panels/alerts: Block rate (region): sum(rate(redwood_residency_enforcement_decision_total{decision="blocked"}[5m])) by (region) / clamp_min(sum(rate(redwood_residency_enforcement_decision_total[5m])) by (region), 1) Top block reasons: topk(10, sum(rate(redwood_residency_enforcement_decision_total{decision="blocked"}[15m])) by (block_reason)) Policy eval p95 (region): histogram_quantile(0.95, sum(rate(redwood_residency_policy_eval_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])) by (le, region)) Cross-region attempts: sum(rate(redwood_residency_cross_region_routing_attempt_total[5m])) by (src_region, dst_region) Notes/assumptions: Metric names match the current instrumentation plan in routing/gateway branches: redwood_residency_enforcement_decision_total (labels: decision, block_reason, region, endpoint, request_type, org_tier, org_id?, project_id?, policy_id?) redwood_residency_policy_eval_duration_seconds_bucket redwood_residency_cross_region_routing_attempt_total redwood_residency_fallback_suppressed_total If labels diverge slightly in final implementation, we can follow up with a small patch; this PR keeps queries isolated in jsonnet so updates are straightforward. Files changed (high level): dashboards/residency/enforcement-overview.jsonnet (new) dashboards/residency/enforcement-drilldowns.jsonnet (new) dashboards/residency/enforcement-errors.jsonnet (new) alerts/residency-enforcement.rules.yml (new) mixins/residency/README.md (new runbook links + definitions) dashboards/_index.jsonnet (register new dashboard) alerts/_index.yml (register new rules) Review notes: Sean Gallagher (SRE): Requested separating page vs ticket alerts and adding minimum volume gates to avoid paging during low-traffic regions. Addressed by adding dual alerts and clamp/volume checks; also added a panel for raw blocked count next to percentage. Rafael Mendes (SRE Manager): Asked for explicit links to runbooks and to tag alerts with the rollout phase label so we can disable paging during Phase 2 staging validation. Added runbook URLs and a required label residency_enforcement_enabled="true" to paging alerts where applicable. Logan Wright (Telemetry/audit): Confirmed that org_id/project_id labels are acceptable in internal metrics; requested a guard to allow redaction in some environments. Implemented by building panels that work with org_tier and optional org_id/project_id. Mina Haddad (Routing): Verified cross-region attempt panels align with how routing will emit src/dst labels; requested a panel to show fallback suppressed vs fallback attempted. Added a small comparison panel. Testing/verification: Rendered dashboards via jsonnet build locally; validated against staging Prometheus with synthetic counters from the residency canary job. Confirmed alert rules load successfully and evaluate with expected firing behavior when replaying a spike scenario. CI: lint-jsonnet: pass prometheus-rules-validate: pass dashboard-render-smoke: pass Outcome: Merged (squash) after approvals; safe to deploy with the next observability-pack rollout. Follow-ups: (1) Once gateway error contract is fully rolled out on streaming endpoints, we should tighten the “generic 5xx on streaming” panel and convert it into an alert for regressions (tracked in ENG-4833). (2) Consider adding an SLO panel (block-rate budget) per allowlisted enterprise org during Phase 3 rollout.
